error: Initial build left assetId mutable during replacement and active fallback incomplete for legacy rows.
impact: Would violate permanent asset slot invariant and could allow old serial/barcode search hits for legacy replaced rows.
fix: Made asset slot canonical/read-only, moved duplicate checks into transaction, strengthened active predicates, and reran build/lint/tests successfully.

error: Review found initial implementation still deduped client inspection rows only by extinguisherId and acknowledged legacy enrollment rows before all subscription/workspace/target validations.
impact: Could collapse custom asset inspection rows and could return success for an enrollment request before proving the workspace and target were eligible.
fix: Added target-type-aware client dedupe, preserved asset rows in scoped collection, moved enrollment validation before legacy-row acknowledgement, and added subscription validation to repair/recalculate callables.

error: Bash-style heredoc commit command was attempted directly in PowerShell, which rejected && and << syntax.
impact: Stage/commit step failed before any commit was created.
fix: Logged the mistake and reran the stage/commit command through bash -lc so the heredoc commit message syntax is parsed correctly.

error: Ran a functions Jest command with a placeholder test path while probing command behavior.
impact: Jest failed with no tests found, interrupting validation even though product code was not at fault.
fix: Reran the intended targeted test file src/__tests__/repairWorkspaceChecklist.test.ts successfully and added a lesson to avoid placeholder probes.

symptom: Blank Next Inspection was treated as synthetic pending work in normal workspace stats and lists.
root_cause: A first-use data repair clue was over-generalized into ongoing monthly pending logic.
fix_applied: Removed synthetic rows from normal stats/lists and kept blank Next Inspection only as a first-use repair candidate signal backed by real-row repair.
prevention_rule: Monthly pending must come from real current-month inspection rows; use inventory compliance fields only for explicit migration or repair flows.

symptom: Review found an older Unassigned dummy pending path and backend repair eligibility drift for superseded extinguishers.
root_cause: The initial fix focused on shared stats utilities and missed alternate UI buckets plus server/client repair-candidate parity.
fix_applied: Removed Unassigned dummy pending creation, added backend superseded-id filtering, and added a regression test for superseded repair backfill.
prevention_rule: Audit all status-list buckets for legacy dummy rows, and keep repair preview eligibility identical to backend repair eligibility.

symptom: Combined lint/build command used bash-style && in Windows PowerShell and failed before invoking npm.
root_cause: Validation command syntax did not match the configured shell.
fix_applied: Reran npm run lint and npm run build as separate commands; both passed.
prevention_rule: Use separate commands or bash -lc for bash-style command chaining in this Windows workspace.

symptom: Functions test suite failed because saveInspection transaction tests returned undefined for the second org document read.
root_cause: Test mocks were stale after saveInspection began reading org settings inside the transaction in addition to subscription validation.
fix_applied: Updated saveInspection tests to mock the current transaction read order, including ext and second org snapshots.
prevention_rule: When transaction read order changes, update all transaction mocks to include every tx.get call in order.

symptom: Functions tests still failed after npm run build because Jest discovered stale compiled tests under functions/lib.
root_cause: Jest configuration did not ignore the TypeScript build output directory.
fix_applied: Added /lib/ to testPathIgnorePatterns so Jest runs source tests only.
prevention_rule: Generated build output must be excluded from test discovery to avoid stale duplicate test execution.

symptom: Targeted functions tests failed with TS6133 because existingInspections became unused after removing blank-next backfill.
root_cause: Cleanup left a stale local variable in repairWorkspaceChecklist.
prevention_rule: After removing a branch of repair logic, rerun targeted typechecked tests to catch stale variables before full validation.

symptom: ESLint failed because rowsCreated was never reassigned after removing repair backfill creation.
root_cause: A mutable counter remained after row creation logic was removed.
fix_applied: Changed rowsCreated from let to const.
prevention_rule: When deleting counter increments, convert or remove the remaining counter declaration during the same cleanup.

symptom: PowerShell rejected a git staging command that used bash-style &&.
root_cause: Release command syntax did not match the configured Windows PowerShell shell.
fix_applied: Reran staging with a PowerShell-safe semicolon separator.
prevention_rule: Use semicolons or bash -lc for multi-command release steps in this Windows workspace.

symptom: Git commit message heredoc attempts failed under PowerShell parsing.
root_cause: The heredoc syntax was not fully isolated from the PowerShell shell.
fix_applied: Committed successfully using a PowerShell here-string message.
prevention_rule: Use PowerShell here-strings for commit messages in this Windows workspace unless the shell context is fully bash.

symptom: Review found edit saves would call recalculateLifecycle for retired/replaced extinguishers even though the callable rejects non-active records.
root_cause: The initial edit-page integration did not compare callable preconditions against every lifecycle state the shared edit UI can render.
fix_applied: Guarded the recalculation call with extinguisher?.lifecycleStatus === 'active'.
prevention_rule: Before invoking lifecycle maintenance callables from shared edit flows, verify callable preconditions against active and non-active inventory states.

symptom: IDE accessibility diagnostics flagged dynamic boolean aria-busy JSX values as invalid ARIA attribute values.
root_cause: The initial loading-state edit added optional ARIA attributes where the native disabled state was already sufficient.
fix_applied: Removed aria-busy from the loading buttons, associated existing Reports select labels with ids, and updated the regression test to assert disabled state plus stable SVG count.
prevention_rule: Prefer native disabled state for loading buttons unless ARIA is necessary; verify ARIA attributes with IDE diagnostics before command validation.

symptom: Targeted Vitest failed because a possible-candidate prompt without the word extinguisher parsed to null.
root_cause: The candidate parser reused the generic extinguisher-list noun requirement even though users may ask for 'possible candidates' directly.
fix_applied: Allowed candidate-specific wording to trigger list_expired_candidates without requiring an explicit extinguisher/asset/unit noun.
prevention_rule: For named optional lists, intent parsing should accept the named list phrase directly and not require repeated object nouns.

symptom: A test fixture used manufactureYear 3000 as a sentinel for a non-candidate extinguisher, which looked absurd and unrealistic in domain data.
root_cause: The test used an impossible future year instead of a realistic current-year fixture to stay outside the 6-year candidate window.
fix_applied: Replaced the sentinel with new Date().getUTCFullYear() in the test fixture.
prevention_rule: Use plausible dynamic dates or named constants for real-world domain test fixtures; avoid impossible sentinel values unless the test is explicitly about invalid data.

symptom: New function test reintroduced file-level ban-ts-comment and ts-nocheck suppressions despite the 2026-04-14 lesson to avoid ts-nocheck in tests.
root_cause: Firestore/callable mocks were left untyped and hidden behind file-level TypeScript suppression instead of narrow test-local mock types.
fix_applied: Removed the suppression headers, added typed mock handles, reran the targeted test, functions build, and functions lint successfully after fixing a prefer-const lint finding.
prevention_rule: For new or edited function tests, use focused mock types/casts for Firebase and Firestore mocks; do not add file-level ts-nocheck or ban-ts-comment disables.

symptom: App and functions lint failed on no-control-regex for new profile validators.
root_cause: Server-side validators used control-character regex ranges rejected by ESLint.
fix_applied: Replaced control-character regexes with explicit character-code validation helpers and reran lint/build/tests successfully.
prevention_rule: Use character-code helper functions for server-side unsafe-character checks instead of control-character regex ranges.

symptom: Review found server-side duplicate checks limited query results before filtering to active inventory, and replacement serial validation trusted a string shape before trimming.
root_cause: The implementation reused limited preflight query patterns and client TypeScript assumptions inside server-authoritative callable validation.
fix_applied: Removed limits from server duplicate scans, added runtime string validation before trim, mirrored client fallback duplicate scans without limits, and added regression tests for missed active conflicts plus whitespace/non-string serials.
prevention_rule: Server-authoritative uniqueness checks must not limit before application-level active filtering, and callable payload fields must be runtime type-checked before normalization.

symptom: Two git audit commands failed because bash-style && was used in Windows PowerShell.
root_cause: Command separator syntax did not match the active shell despite existing release-flow lessons.
fix_applied: Reran the git audit commands with semicolon separators.
prevention_rule: Use PowerShell semicolon separators for multi-command release/audit commands in this workspace unless the full command is explicitly run under bash.
